observations.recommendation:
1.32 We reiterated our recommendation with modifications and the Local Chief
Executive agreed to instruct the:
a. Inventory Committee to (i) conduct a complete physical inventory and
submit the Report on the Physical Count of Property, Plant and
Equipment to the office of the Audit Team not later than January 31 of
each year; and (ii) ensure the proper tagging of all properties and adopt a
standardized system of property numbering;
b. Acting GSO to (i) maintain Property Cards based on source documents,
such as Purchase Orders, Delivery Receipts/Invoices, and Inspection and
Acceptance Reports, ensuring that all required details are accurately and
completely recorded for each type of property; (ii) utilize Annex C – List
38
of Non-Existing/Missing Property, Plant, and Equipment (PPE), as
prescribed under COA Circular No. 2020-006; and (iii) maintain Local
Road Inventory and Road Map and Local Road Network Property Card;
c. Municipal Accountant to complete all required details of the Property,
Plant and Equipment Ledger Card (PPELC);
d. Municipal Engineering Office to provide the Municipal Accountant and
the Acting GSO the complete description and cost segregation of road
components for road projects as basis of the Municipal Accountant to
record the same in the books of accounts; and
e. Municipal Assessor to immediately survey the landholdings declared
under the name of the Municipality and assist the Acting General Services
Officer to undertake the necessary actions for the registration and titling
of the landholdings.
1.33 We further recommended and the management agreed to coordinate with the
DPWH Pampanga 1st District Engineering Office to obtain the necessary
documents of transfer and henceforth recognize all the completed
infrastructure projects in the Municipality’s books. Additionally, exert utmost
effort to follow up with the City Government of Mabalacat, the Deed of
Donation, Property Transfer Report and original copies of the Official Receipt
and Certificate of Registration to facilitate proper recognition thereof.

8.11 We reiterated our recommendations that management initiate strict control
measures in reviewing the supporting documents of the payments of salaries to job
order personnel.

9.10 We recommended and the Local Chief Executive agreed that the Municipal
Accountant require all employees, officials, and disbursing officers granted cash
advances to prepare and submit a liquidation report or report of disbursements in
support of the liquidation of the cash advances granted to them.

10.9 We recommended and the Municipal Planning and Development Officer,
together with the GAD Focal Person and TWG, agreed to prioritize the establishment
of a GAD database containing gender-disaggregated data across relevant sectors that
will serve as basis for gender-responsive planning, programming and policy
formulation.

12.8 However, with regard to past due accounts reflected in the GSIS-eBCS, we
recommended and the Local Chief Executive agreed to instruct the Accounting Office
to continuously coordinate and follow up with the GSIS until all discrepancies
between the Municipality's records and those of the GSIS are fully resolved.

13.7 We commended the management for the proper withholding of personnel
premium contributions and loan repayments, and the prompt remittance thereof,
including the government shares within the prescribed period pursuant to R.A. No.
9679 and its Implementing Rules and Regulations (IRR) in relation to HDMF
Circular No. 275. Nevertheless, we recommended and the Accounting Office agreed
to continue to strictly observe the requirements of both issuances.

14.7 We commended Management for its consistent adherence to existing laws,
rules, and regulations on the proper deduction and remittance of PhilHealth
contributions for its employees and personnel, in accordance with R.A. No. 10606, as
amended. Nevertheless, we recommended and management agreed to continue the
observance of these provisions to sustain the benefits for its employees.

15.8 We commended the Management for its accurate withholding and prompt
remittance of taxes to the BIR. Nonetheless, we recommended and management
agreed to continue the strict adherence to proper withholding and timely remittance
in accordance with BIR RR Nos. 11-2018 dated January 31, 2018 and 2-98 dated May
17, 1998, including RMC No. 23-2012 dated February 14, 2012.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended the following courses of action:

a. The Municipal Treasurer

i. Prepare a stratified schedule of “presumptive income level” to approximate the gross receipt of each business classification for every tax period;

ii. Require all persons, who are granted a permit to conduct an activity or business and who are subject to payment of business tax to submit a certified photocopy of their income tax returns (ITR) on or before April 30 of each year and collect any tax deficiency, if any, including interest at the rate of ten percent (10%) corresponding to the two percent (2%) per month from January to May. Otherwise, enforce the corresponding penalty for non-compliance therewith;

iii. Enjoin business establishments to pay taxes prior to retirement/closure; and

iv. Collect the tax deficiency from the concerned business entities; and

v. Enforce other remedies to ensure that the correct amount of fees and taxes is collected.

b. The designated Business Permit and Licensing Officer ensure that applicants, (i) for newly started businesses or renewal of existing permits, submit a complete set of all documentary requirements in securing permits and licenses prior to the approval of the Local Chief Executive; and (ii) declare the correct amount of gross sales in their application consistent with the provision of the Updated Revenue Ordinance.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Local Chief Executive, thru the MENRO-designate and his subordinates:

a. enjoin all barangays to support the solid waste management program of the municipality through continued efforts to educate their respective constituents on the proper segregation of reusable or recyclable materials from compostable wastes; strict enforcement of “No Segregation, No Collection Policy”; and establishment of functional MRF in all barangays or clustered MRF among barangays;

b. strengthen the implementation of existing ordinances and policies on solid waste management at the barangay level, specifically the required Personal Protective Equipment (PPE) and training on proper handling of waste, in order to achieve a community compliance and eventually abide by the provisions on preserving and protecting the environment required under R.A. No. 9003; and

c. confer with the MSWMB to include the estimated annual garbage fees to be collected as fund source in the preparation of its annual investment plan and submit the same for annual budget deliberation in order to appropriate garbage fees to the operations of the solid waste management.

observations.recommendation:
1.37 We reiterated our recommendations with modifications and the Local
Chief Executive agreed to instruct the:
a. Inventory Committee to conduct a complete physical inventory and
submit the Report on the Physical Count of Property, Plant and
Equipment not later than January 31 of each year;
b. Acting GSO to (i) maintain Property Cards for each type of property
in coordination with the Office of the Municipal Accountant; (ii) issue
the corresponding Acknowledgement Receipt for Equipment (ARE)
to fix the accountability of the end-user over the property; and (iii)
maintain Local Road Inventory and Road Map and Local Road
Network Property Card;
c. Representative of the office receiving any property from any source to
secure all turnover documents or deed of donation from the donor and
furnish the Accounting and GSO Office a copy for proper recognition
of the same in the books of accounts simultaneous with the fixing of
accountability therefor;
d. Municipal Accountant to (i) prepare and maintain Property, Plant
and Equipment Ledger Card (PPELC), Construction-in-Progress
Ledger Cards (CIPLC) and Real Property Ledger Cards (RPLC); (ii)
recognize donated properties in the books of accounts; and (iii) draw
the necessary journal entry voucher/s to reflect the adjustments on
depreciation and misclassified PPEs;
e. Municipal Engineering Office to provide the Municipal Accountant
and the Acting GSO the complete description and cost segregation of
road components for road projects and, on the basis of which, the
Municipal Accountant to record the same in the books of accounts;
and
f. Municipal Assessor to immediately survey the landholdings declared
under the name of the Municipality and assist the Municipal
Treasurer (or General Services Officer) in undertaking the necessary
actions for the registration and titling of the landholdings.

2.35 We recommended that the Local Chief Executive instruct the:
(a) Municipal Engineer, being the building official, to inform all
applicants of building permit of their responsibilities after the
completion of the structure which include, among others, the filing of
a sworn declaration of the true value of the structure;
(b) Municipal Assessor to declare all identified undeclared property in
the name of the defaulting owner, if known, or against an unknown
owner, as the case may be, and assess the property for taxation in
accordance with laws, rules and regulations;
(c) Municipal Treasurer to (i) ensure that the Municipal Accountant will
be furnished with a copy of complete reports of collections from the
Provincial Government of Pampanga (PGP); and (ii) regularly
prepare and issue the necessary notice of delinquency and demand
letters to delinquent taxpayers; and
(d) Municipal Accountant to (a) secure a report of RPT collected by the
PGP every year-end as the basis for the recognition of Receivables and
Income accruing to the Municipality; (b) ensure that RPT/SET
Receivables and Deferred Charges are duly adjusted upon
receipt/remittance of Municipal share from the PGP; and (c)
recognize in the books all adjusting and correcting entries.

3.46 However, we recommended and the Local Chief Executive agreed to direct
the:
a. Local Development Council to (i) specify the projects, programs and
activities under the Development fund; (ii) stop the practice of lump
sum budgeting and inclusion of generic items in the Annual
Investment Plan and Annual Budget, otherwise secure prior authority
from the Local Sanggunian; and (iii) ensure that the development
projects to be funded out of the 20% DF are well planned and
procurement and implementation ready;
b. Chairman and Members of the BAC and TWG to (i) carefully
scrutinize the documentary requirements submitted by the bidder and
conduct verification thereof to ascertain any commitment by the latter
and prevent any conflicting information thereon; and (ii) ensure the
submission by the winning bidder of a valid performance security
prior to signing of the contract to safeguard the government from any
untoward act of the former, if there be any;
c. Municipal Engineer to (i) conduct close monitoring during project
implementation and ensure compliance by the contractor with the
65
requirements of the contract; and (ii) prepare variation order should
a change of work from the original plan occur; and
d. Municipal Accountant, in close coordination with the BAC, to (i)
subject progress payments to the retention fee of ten percent (10%)
until fifty percent (50%) completion of the value of works provided it
is satisfactorily done and on schedule; (ii) submit all the necessary
supporting documents for the identified contracts; and (iii) verify all
supporting documents as to completeness and see to it that the same
are attached to the disbursement vouchers before effecting payments
thereof.

5.29 We reiterated our recommendations that Management:
a. thru the Chairman and Members of the BAC and TWG and the
Municipal Engineer, (i) diligently observe the required timeframe for
each procurement activity as stated in the IRR of R.A. No. 9184; (ii)
ensure the submission by the winning bidder of a valid performance
security prior to signing of the contract to safeguard the government
from untoward act of the former, if there be any; and (iii) ensure that
the commencement of awarded contract will start on or after the
issuance of the Notice to Proceed as the terms of the contract will
become effective only at the time of receipt thereof by the successful
bidder; and
b. thru the Head of the Accounting Unit, (i) apply the provisions
governing the retention money; and (ii) recognize the asset and
corresponding liability account for goods received by and services
rendered to the municipality to facilitate fair presentation of the
financial statements.

6.8 We reiterated our recommendations and the Local Chief Executive agreed
to (a) consider the aforecited rules and regulations on the hiring of JO
personnel; (b) strictly implement control measures on the required
submission of DTRs by service providers prior to the actual day of payout
as basis in the preparation of payrolls; (c) avoid hiring of JO personnel
whose functions either overlap or duplicate with those of the regular
employees or are considered recurring, necessary and essential to the
Municipality; and (d) ensure that the JO personnel who have reached the
compulsory retirement age of 65 are occupationally qualified based on the
positions applied for or offered.

7.28 We reiterated our previous year’s recommendations with modification
that the Local Chief Executive instruct the:
a. concerned Barangays to establish their own MRFs pursuant to the
provisions of R.A. No. 9003 to aid the reduction of mixed wastes;
b. MENRO-designate and his subordinates to (i) stop accepting and
accommodating wastes not considered residual from the barangays;
(ii) strengthen the implementation of existing ordinances and policies
on solid waste management at the barangay level in order to achieve
a community compliance and eventually abide by the provisions on
preserving and protecting the environment required under R.A. No.
9003; and (iii) stop the practice of burning solid wastes; and
c. MSWMB to initiate the preparation of its annual investment plan
based on the estimated annual garbage fees to be collected and submit
the same for annual budget deliberation in order to appropriate fees
to the operations of the solid waste management.

8.11 We recommended that the Local Chief Executive (a) direct the Municipal
Planning and Development Officer, together with the GAD Focal Person
and TWG, to update the GAD database which will serve as basis for
gender-responsive planning, programming and policy formulation; and
(b) ensure that GAD mechanisms and processes are continuously in place
to sustain the LGU’s critical consciousness in supporting gender and
development pursuant to PCW-DILG-DBM-NEDA JMC Nos. 2013-01
and 2016-01.

9.11 However, for past due accounts based on the GSIS-EBCS, we
recommended and the Local Chief Executive agreed to:
a) instruct the Accounting Office to consistently confer with the GSIS
until the discrepancies between the records of the Municipality and
the GSIS are resolved; and
b) require the concerned official and/or employee to settle or restructure
their arrears.

Management Comment: The Municipal Treasurer committed to regularly prepare required report of checks issued and report of unleased checks, and forward the same to the Municipal Accountant as basis for the recognition in the books of accounts.

We recommended that the:
LDRRM Officer (i) prepare the LDRRMFIP in accordance with COA Circular 2012-002 showing, among others, the allocation of funds for every PPA, schedule of implementation, implementing office, expected output, funding source and expense class; and (ii) include under a separate caption, the list of projects and activities charged to the unexpended LDRRMF of previous years;
Management ensure that any change in the annual budget is supported with valid authority and documentation pursuant to Section 321 of R.A. 7160 and Section 454(2)(b) of its IRR; and
Municipal Accountant prepare a note to financial statements pursuant to Section 5.1.16 of COA Circular 2012-002 to inform the users of the financial statement of the status of the LDRRMF as of reporting period.
Management Comment: The LDRRMO committed to prepare the necessary LDRRMFIP in accordance with the regulation. The Municipal Accountant on the other hand, furnished the Audit Team of a sample note that will form part of the financial statements of the municipality in the ensuing period.

We recommended and the Local Chief Executive agreed to direct the:
Chairman and Members of the BAC and TWG to (i) carefully scrutinize the documentary requirements submitted by the bidder and conduct verification thereof to ascertain any commitment by the bidder and prevent any conflicting information thereon; and (ii) ensure the submission by the winning bidder of a valid performance security prior to signing of the contract to safeguard the government from any untoward act of the former, if there be any;
Municipal Engineer to conduct close monitoring during project implementation and ensure compliance by the contractor with the requirements of the contract; and
Municipal Accountant, in close coordination with the BAC and Municipal Engineer, to (i) submit all the necessary supporting documents for the identified contracts; and (ii) verify all supporting documents as to completeness and see to it that the same are attached to the disbursement vouchers before effecting payments thereof.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive instruct the HRMO to (a) consider the aforecited rules and regulations on the hiring of service providers; (b) strictly implement control measures on the required submission of DTRs by service providers prior to actual day of payout as basis in the preparation of payrolls; (c) avoid hiring of service providers whose functions either (i) overlap or duplicate with those of the regular employees or (ii) are considered recurring, necessary, and essential to the Municipality; (d) encourage the service providers to take and pass the Civil Service Exam for them to be qualified and be filled in as regular or permanent employees of the Municipality by providing them free review and payment fee for such exam; and (e) ensure that the service providers, who have reached the compulsory retirement age of 65, are occupationally qualified based on the positions applied for or offered.
Management Comment: The HRM Head will require and implement the submission of DTRs, three (3) days before the actual day of payout as basis in the preparation of payrolls. The municipality will appropriate in its 2023 budget for the free review and examination fees of Job Order personnel to encourage and equip them to take the Civil Service Examination.
Meanwhile, to augment the shortage in personnel per office, the Municipality will resort to hiring of service providers.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive:
through the MENRO and Liga ng mga Barangay, (i) encourage the active participation of barangay officials to support the solid waste management program of the government by educating their respective constituents on the proper segregation of reusable or recyclable materials from compostable wastes and coordinate efforts in the implementation of the five basic environmental laws; and (ii)  strengthen the implementation of existing ordinances and policies on solid waste management at the barangay level in order to achieve a community compliance and eventually abide by the provisions on preserving and protecting the environment required under R.A. No. 9003;
ensure that the MRF is secured against unauthorized access; and
as far as the Municipal budget permits, provide necessary support in enjoining barangays of the municipality to establish their own or clustered MRF.
Management Comment: For the time being, though costly, the Municipality maintained a functional Central Materials Recovery Facility to ensure that a 100% waste collection efficiency is attained so as not to prejudice the condition and interest of the populace on the aspect of public health and environment hazards.
On the other hand, the rotary composter & bio-waste shredder and its accessories are both components vital in producing compost materials (fertilizer) out of biodegradable waste which in turn will be considered as an initial waste diversion into useable output/product, thus eventually will reduce waste intended for disposal at sanitary landfill and the cost component thereof.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive:
in his capacity as Chairman of the GAD Focal Point System (GFPS), consider the amendment of the composition or reconstitution of the GFPS-TWG to include all other mandatory members thereof and create the M&E Team to facilitate the monitoring and evaluation of gender mainstreaming efforts of the Municipality;
direct the Municipal Planning and Development Officer, together with the GAD Focal Person and TWG, to exert effort in establishing a GAD database which will serve as basis for gender-responsive planning, programming and policy formulation; and
instruct the GAD FPS to (i) continually comply with the GAD mechanisms and processes to sustain the LGU’s critical consciousness in supporting gender and development, women’s empowerment and responding to gender issues pursuant to PCW-DILG-DBM-NEDA JMC Nos. 2013-01 and 2016-01; and (ii) submit the documentary requirements supporting the GAD Accomplishment Report.

We commended Management for the prompt remittance of the taxes withheld during the current year to the BIR. This notwithstanding, we recommended and the LCE agreed to continually ensure the withholding and timely remittance of taxes in conformity with Revenue Memorandum Circular Nos. 23-007 and 23-2012, and BIR Revenue Regulation No. 2-98.

We commended Management for the faithful compliance with the withholding and prompt remittance of premiums to the PhilHealth and recommended the continual observance thereof for the benefits of its employees.

We commended Management in complying with the provisions of R.A. No. 9679 on the withholding and prompt remittance of contributions and loan amortizations, and advised the continued observance of the requirements of the Act.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended and management agreed that 
(a) Municipal Planning and Development Officer (MPDO) initiate the establishment and maintenance of the GAD database to serve as basis for gender-responsive planning, programming and policy formulation; and 
(b) GAD focal point to monitor the full implementation of the programmed activities for GAD.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local Chief Executive (a) augment, as far as possible, the composition of the inventory committee to expedite the completion of the  the  physical inventory process and pave the way for the preparation of Report on the Physical Count of Property, Plant and Equipment (RPCPPE) that is duly reconciled with the accounting and property records and submission to the Auditor’s Office; (b) require the Municipal Accountant and the designated Property Officer to (i) continue the provision of subsidiary ledgers for PPE items by aiming for the full completion of the current and a percentage of recent year’s acquisition until all PPE items are covered; (ii) reconcile the respective records of the Municipal Accountant and the designated Property Officer relative to the PPE balances; (c) create an appraisal committee for the un-booked parcels of land and record the same under the PPE account; and (d) require the Municipal Treasury, Supply Officer and the Municipal Assessor to ascertain the status of parcels of land owned by the agency with titling issues and work for the titling thereof in the name of the Municipality of Apalit, assuming funds have been provided thereof.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive, Sangguniang Bayan and Local Development Council strictly comply with the Memorandum of Agreement agreed upon by both parties (Funding agencies and the implementing agency) and ensure the effective and efficient implementation of the proposed projects within their targeted completion period through close monitoring and supervision to maximize the developmental benefits and the early enjoyment thereof by the intended beneficiaries.

We still recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the
Municipal Social Welfare Officer to ensure that the nutritional status of the minimal number of under-nourished children improve to normal and exert more efforts to maintain or improve further the number of children within the normal weight; and
As far as practicable, considering the maintaining balance requirement of the account, Municipal Treasurer to comply with Section 2.4 of DSWD Administrative Order No. 08 Series of 2012 on the opening of a separate bank account for the Program.

We recommended that the Members of the Municipal Local School Board:
prepare a balanced budget with conscious and concerted efforts of observing compliance with the legal and regulatory requirements on SEF budgeting, allocation and utilization as laid down in the latest guidelines under DepEd, DBM & DILG Joint Circular No. 1 s. of 2017 which specifically identified that the repair and construction of classrooms shall be a priority item in the SEF budget over other eligible items; and
request for inputs from the DepEd official/School Division Superintendent in the prioritization of SEF projects and expenditures in accordance with the DepEd criteria and with due regard to the provisions of laws, rules and regulations such as the latest  guidelines in DepEd, DBM & DILG Joint Circular No. 1 s. of 2017 .

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive, Sangguniang Bayan and Local Development Council strictly comply with DILG-DBM Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2011-1 dated April 11, 2011 and (a) invest in high-impact capital expenditures projects in the utilization of its 20% Development Fund  and refrain from proposing non-capital investment projects under the Fund;  and (b) ensure the effective and efficient implementation of the proposed projects within their targeted completion period through close monitoring and supervision to maximize the developmental benefits and the early enjoyment thereof by the intended beneficiaries.

We commended management of its efforts to achieve effective planning, implementation and monitoring of the Agency’s GAD PPAs.  However, we recommended that the (a) GAD focal person initiate the establishment and maintenance of the GAD database to serve as basis for gender-responsive planning, programming and policy formulation; and (b) GAD board re-evaluate the achievement of objectives thru the full implementation of programmed GAD PPAs before augmenting the same to other PPAs, and (c) abide by the schedule on GAD Planning and budgeting provided by Joint Memorandum Circular 2012-001.

We recommended that the General Services Officer procure, as far as practicable and feasible, directly from the PS-DBM all anticipated common-use supplies, and resort to local suppliers only upon unavailability of supplies needed using the prescribed alternative modes of procurement.

We recommend that the Municipal Accountant (a) draw the appropriate journal entry to correct the erroneous classification of the property items as expense instead of as assets; and (b) perform reasonable review in the work of the staff for accuracy and correctness of the journal entries being passed; and (c) be guided by the definition of accounts in the Revised Chart of Accounts for LGUS in the use of the appropriate account title for fair presentation of accounts in the financial statements.

We recommended that the General Services Officer and other officials/offices concerned furnish the Audit Team with copies of contracts/purchase orders and supporting documents and Inspection and Acceptance Reports as required by COA Circular Nos. 2009-001 and 95-006. This is designed to enable the audit team to perform its duties relating to post audit of the transactions, at the most appropriate time and manner without delaying or obstructing the implementation or prosecution of the project.

We recommended that the:
Members of the SP re-enact appropriate ordinance to prevent  undesirable outcomes as a result of unregulated  proliferation of tricycles along the national highway to  improve flow of traffic and promote public safety and order;
Secretary to the SP ensure that original and back-up copies of all ordinances are properly archived and filed for ready reference.
Local Chief Executive, thru his Administrator and the Traffic Management Head (i) with proper consultation with stakeholders, design  the re-routing plan for the tricycles from normally using the McArthur Highway to the one using  the alternative routes to ease the flow of traffic along the national highway; (ii) strictly direct all enforcers/personnel  under his command to apprehend those traffic violators, sidewalk vendors and also vendors who occupy sidewalk as extension to their vending place; (iii) assist the affected vendors in scouting for allowable area where they sell their wares or put up the stalls; and (iv) ensure the effective enforcement of the Ordinance banning the operation of tricycles-for-hire along the national road.

We commended the LCE and MDRRMO Officer and its staff of their efforts to comply with the provisions of the Implementing Rules and Regulations (IRR) of Republic Act No. 10121, otherwise known as the Philippine Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Act of 2010 and other related rules/regulations/laws regarding disasters preparedness.  However, we recommended that the Local Chief Executive require:
a. 	the MDRRMO to continue efforts to train, research on and conduct planning to improve further competencies in  operations and warning to effectively and efficiently address the requirements of the existing rules and regulations on disaster preparedness, mitigation, recovery and response;
b.    as head of the MDC, to fast track the preparation of the Municipality’s Comprehensive Development Plan (CDP) and Comprehensive Land-Use Plan (CLUP) in order that disaster risk management activities could be integrated to the development plan of the Municipality to prevent further loss of lives and properties; and
Members of the MDRRMC ensure that the member CSOs/NGOs to the Council possess the eligibility requirements cited under NDRRMC No.  3 to assure of effective and strengthened citizen’s participation in the locality.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive (a) through the Liga ng mga Barangay, encourage active participation of barangay officials to support the solid waste management program of the government by educating their respective constituents on the proper segregation of reusable or recyclable materials from compostable wastes; (b) through the ESMO, comply with the requirements of the law on waste segregation by placing trash bins with proper labels and by collecting only those garbage that are properly segregated; (c) ensure that the existing MRF of the five component barangays are fully functional and provide necessary support in enjoining the remaining seven barangays to establish their own MRF; (d) recommend to the Sangguniang Bayan (SB) policies and possible measures that will ensure full compliance by the barangays on the provisions of R.A. No. 9003; (e) initiate the passage of Anti-Littering policy ordinances as provided by Section 48 of R.A. No. 9003 for those barangays that had existing policy; (f) create a new Municipal Solid Waste Management Board comprised of officials who could actively perform their respective duties and perform their functions as intended; (g) review every two years or as the need arises the Municipal Solid Waste  Management Plan for the purposes of ensuring its sustainability, viability, effectiveness and relevance in relation to local and international developments in the field of solid waste management (h) provide support to the concerned barangay for the construction of secured fence for the MRF for security, health and sanitation purposes;  (i) relocate those in the nearby area safe from hazardous elements; and (i)  address with dispatch the issue concerning the MRF near the slaughterhouse to protect the health of the residents.
We recommended that the Municipal officials continue to comply with the provisions of the LGC on the allowable disbursements pertaining to leagues and federation of local elective officials.

We commended the officials and employees concerned for being compliant with the GSIS regulations and recommended that they continue being compliant in the deduction and remittance of the correct amounts within the prescribed period.

We recommended and management agreed that the Municipal General Services Officer abide by the requirements of the NGAS Manual on the purchase, recognition and issuances of Supplies and Property, provided they are not contrary to the PPSAS on inventories.
